dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3, respond for a call related to acute lower abdominal pain and reported blood in stool.
Hi, my name's Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What brought you to call for help today?
I've had lower abdominal pain with dark red blood in my stool for the past couple of days.
Have you noticed other symptoms, such as vomiting or changes in your bowel movements?
I vomited once yesterday with diarrhea and blood-tinged stool, but not since.
You said you had a similar episode three weeks ago. What was determined back then?
It was a bladder infection at that time.
Are you alert and responsive to questions and stimulation?
You're opening your eyes completely. Are you responding appropriately now?
You seem oriented and answering clearly to verbal input.
Please point both hands toward me one at a time and move your head toward me when I nod.
(Complies with hand and head movement.)
You are fully alert and oriented with a GCS of 15-eye 4, verbal 5, motor 6.
I'm checking your airway and confirming there is no obstruction. No issues found so far.
Your breathing is regular at 18 breaths per minute and appears unlabored.
Are you experiencing any cyanosis or unusual sounds during breathing?
No, I haven't noticed those.
I need to feel your pulse and check for signs of poor perfusion. Can I take your wrist here?
Pulse is strong at 106, and skin appears flushed but not pale or cyanotic.
Blood pressure is elevated at 171/95. Has this been typical for you?
Respiratory rate is 18 per minute-normal but slightly increased.
Pulse remains at 106 beats per minute.
Blood glucose is 173-this is elevated and fits your known diabetic status.
Oxygen saturation reading is 96 percent on ambient air.
4-lead EKG shows sinus tachycardia, no signs of acute MI.
When did the pain first begin and how is it best described?
It started two days ago as a sharp, deep ache lower in my abdomen.
Have you noticed anything that makes the pain worse or better?
It has been constant; moves don't help and it won't go away.
Do you have any known allergies to medications or substances?
I don't have any known allergies.
What medications are you currently using to manage your health?
Taking Clonidine, famotidine, and metformin on a regular schedule.
Any prior issues you want me to know such as diabetes or high blood pressure?
I have type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and GERD as known issues.
When was your last meal or fluids before the pain started?
I just had coffee around 3 a.m. the other day.
How has the pain changed or progressed since it began?
It stayed about the same, but never went away. It's always there.
When exactly did the acute phase of the pain begin?
Right around 2 days ago without a clear trigger, I think.
Have you noticed anything causing or worsening the pain?
Not really-no position or food makes it any better at any time.
What words would best describe the type of pain you're feeling?
It's a sharp, stabbing pain more than a dull one.
Has it traveled or spread to other areas of your body?
It stays localized-only in my lower abdomen.
How severe is it using a 1-10 scale where 10 is the worst?
It's at 10, exactly. It never lets up.
Has the pain increased, decreased, or remained steady over time?
It has been consistent throughout-no real change.
I'll now review your skin for signs like rashes or swelling.
No bruising, scars, or rashes are present, and turgor remains normal.
Temperature from inner right calf feels warm; skin appears reddened under stress.
Next, I'll evaluate your head, eyes, and neck for any issues.
Are you experiencing any headaches or visual issues right now?
No, just the abdominal pain from before.
You've been alert with A&O x 4-time, place, person, and situation.
Your ears, nose, and mouth appear clear with no discharge or blockage.
No chest signs, but I'll quickly check your heart and lungs.
Bilateral breath sounds are normal with no wheeze or crackles.
Heart rhythm is clear, consistent with the EKG from earlier.
There's guarding when I press over the lower right area-does that hurt?
Yes, it hurts more in that area, around the lower right region.
Your abdomen has no visible distention and normal bowel sounds.
Have you experienced any nausea or vomiting recently?
Some nausea yes, but only one episode of vomiting recently.
You have rectal bleeding with dark red stool-describe how often it occurs.
Diarrhea comes with the blood about since the pain began.
I'll now check your upper and lower extremities for signs of injury or swelling.
No apparent deformities, angulation, or known motor issues on review.
Pulse is still elevated at 109 and increasing slightly over time.
Your breaths increased to 20 per minute, consistent with stress.
Blood pressure remains high at 174/88, but trending slightly upwards.
Glucose dropped to 0-possibly due to lack of intake and vomiting.
Oxygen levels are at 96 percent and slightly improved since earlier baseline.
12-lead EKG reaffirms sinus tach with T wave changes but no cardiac signs.
Given poor access, attempting IV is not possible at this time.
Continuous 4-lead monitoring will help track any rapid changes.
Per your vitals and presentation, Patient is under Protocol 3-2 for abdominal pain.
Repeat vitals confirm trends-BP 195/77, pulse 112, stable breathing.
Pain remains at 10/10, focused to lower right and unchanging in features.
No new information noted on repeat history review.
VCU ED has been selected for ED receive and continued management.
Patient has been transferred and secured in semi-Fowler's position for transport.
Patient is being transported in a cold condition due to active GI bleed signs.
She has been in distress since initial contact, with guarding showing persistent pain.
Earlier history includes vomiting, a potential sign of GI tract bleeding.
Nausea was reported once and may be related to the ongoing medical condition.
